read.dek: The episode's pressure is not that religion sometimes decorates politics. It is that end-times stories can become scripts for coordination: they give people roles, tell them what catastrophe means, and make separate factions move toward the same Middle East furnace.
read.thesis: Eschatology is treated as a technology of coordination. It captures imagination, supplies a future, rewards believers materially, and lets people cooperate without an explicit conspiracy. From that model Jiang builds a 10-to-20-year forecast: Iran hardens, America overreaches, Dugin's Eurasian plan advances, Catholic and Orthodox projects collide with liberal modernity, and the same apocalyptic convergence points keep pulling geopolitics back to Israel, Islam, Russia, and the ruins of Western strategic time.

opening.heading: Core Reading
opening.text: The central move is to stop treating apocalypse as a private belief and start reading it as a public script. A script has roles. A script has momentum. A script lets people coordinate before they have admitted to themselves that they are coordinating. That is why eschatology matters here: not because every believer is a fanatic, but because the fanatics with the most energy can make the story politically real.

beats.summary: The episode opens by recoding the apparent pause in U.S.-Iran escalation as hidden hybrid war.

paragraphs.text: The first answer to why World War III has not started is that the visible battlefield is the wrong instrument. Modern war moves through media control, cyber pressure, psychological weakening, sanctions, covert action, leadership targeting, and sabotage. The war can be underway before it has the shape the audience expects.

paragraphs.text: An eschatology is powerful because it is complete. It answers where we came from, what we are doing here, and where we are going. More importantly, it gives people parts to play. The believer is not only waiting for the end; the believer is auditioning for a role inside a story that already knows where history is supposed to go.

Judaism receives the structure as a messiah of war who restores Israel, gathers the diaspora, builds the Third Temple, and ushers in peace and justice. Protestant Christian Zionism reworks the sequence around Jesus' return, the rapture, the Antichrist, Gog and Magog, and the millennium.

America is somehow absent: defeated in Iran, trapped in civil war, or otherwise no longer acting as hegemon. China is absent too, weakened by ecological, food, water, resource, and internal stability problems even as it later becomes dominant in the Middle East.

beats.summary: The Anglo-American empire's lack of counterstrategy is blamed on an aging ruling class with no stake in the aftermath.
